_note: This is a SCHEMA EXAMPLE showing the structure of a Triad Engine domain guide. The actual Rome 110 CE guide used in this benchmark is proprietary. You can build your own guide for any domain using this structure.
overview: A 2-3 paragraph overview of the domain setting, establishing time, place, and cultural context.

time_period_context.setting: Exact year and ruling authority (e.g. '110 CE, during the reign of Emperor Trajan')
time_period_context.ruler: Name and key facts about the current ruler/authority
time_period_context.currency: Monetary system with denominations and relative values

not_yet_built: Buildings, monuments, or infrastructure that do not exist yet in your time period
Example: Hadrian's Wall (not started until 122 CE)
not_yet_happened: Events that have not occurred yet
Example: Trajan's Parthian War (not until 114 CE)
already_dead: Historical figures who have already died by your time period
technology_not_available: Technologies, materials, or objects that do not exist in your period
Example: No stirrups, no paper (papyrus/wax tablets instead), no glass windows (wooden shutters)

location_name.historical_use: What this location was actually used for (prevents venue confusion)
location_name.who_goes_here: Which social classes frequent this location

class_name.clothing: What this class wears — specific garments, colors, materials
class_name.clothing_restrictions: What this class does NOT wear (e.g. freedmen don't wear togas)

daily_life.clothing_by_class: Detailed clothing descriptions per social class — this is critical for image accuracy
daily_life.furniture_and_objects: What furniture and household objects actually existed
daily_life.grooming: Hair styles, cosmetics, mirrors, tools — all period-specific

customs.greetings_and_gestures: How people greet, shake hands (or don't), show respect
customs.religious_practices: What religious ceremonies actually looked like (not modern assumptions)
customs.business_customs: How trade and commerce were conducted

characters.name: Character Name
characters.appearance: Physical description for image generation consistency
characters.clothing_details: Exact garments, colors, accessories — specific to their social class
characters.key_visual_markers: The 2-3 details that visually distinguish this character
